summary: Coroutine bridge is hard to test due to blocking wrapper and real delay usage.

findings.evidence: refreshRemote uses real delay and refreshBlocking wraps it in runBlocking, pushing tests toward real-time waiting.
findings.minimal_fix: Inject dispatcher/test scheduler and expose suspend API for core logic; keep blocking bridge as thin adapter.
findings.verification: Add coroutine tests that advance virtual time and verify refresh result without Thread.sleep.

findings.evidence: Current shape encourages slow tests around blocking calls instead of focused suspend-function tests.
findings.minimal_fix: Test refreshRemote with test dispatcher and keep one lightweight integration test for blocking adapter wiring.
findings.verification: Run the suite, measure runtime, and verify unit tests complete without real-time delay dependency.
